t_ch: 小小灰，那边才不是明天！
t_en: Little Gray, you won't find tomorrow there!

t_ch: 灾厄的长臂只会掣肘懦弱的人，你是弱者吗？
t_en: Only the weak will be hindered by the far-reaching grasp of Calamity. Are you one of them?

t_ch: 搭档，别输给死亡的阴霾！
t_en: Partner, don't you dare lose to the shadows of death!
(Finally...)

t_ch: （…谢谢你，遐蝶。）
t_en: (...Thank you, Castorice.)

t_ch: （可是，已经到终点了。也许，我还能最后再看一眼这片花海……）
t_en: (Since I've already made it to the end, maybe I can have one last glance at that sea of flowers...)

t_ch: <color=#ff3a3e>不要回头，向前看。</color>
t_en: <color=#ff3a3e>Don't turn around. Keep looking forward.</color>

t_ch: <color=#ff3a3e>不要回头，向前看。</color>
t_en: <color=#ff3a3e>Don't turn around. Keep looking forward.</color>

t_ch: <color=#ff3a3e>不要回头，向前看。</color>
t_en: <color=#ff3a3e>Don't turn around. Keep looking forward.</color>

t_ch: <color=#ff3a3e>不要回头，向前看。</color>
t_en: <color=#ff3a3e>Don't turn around. Keep looking forward.</color>

t_ch: <color=#ff3a3e>不要回头，向前看。</color>
t_en: <color=#ff3a3e>Don't turn around. Keep looking forward.</color>
(What's this...!?)

t_ch: <color=#ff3a3e>不要害怕。在那道路的尽头，始终会有一抹红色。</color>
t_en: <color=#ff3a3e>Fear not, as a touch of red will be awaiting you at the end of the path.</color>

t_ch: <color=#ff3a3e>它将为你指引出路。如此…你们一定能够在阳光下相聚。</color>
t_en: <color=#ff3a3e>It'll guide you and illuminate the way out. By doing so... you'll eventually reunite in the warmth of the sunlight.</color>

t_ch: （好险，差点就……）
t_en: (That was close. I almost...)

t_ch: （这些身影和声音，究竟是…？）
t_en: (What in the world are these shadows and voices...?)

t_ch: 「这一切，就是你曾经活过的证明。」
t_en: "All of this is proof that you once lived."

t_ch: 「你的灵魂、记忆、曾走过的岁月，和{RUBY_B#岁月之泰坦，欧洛尼斯}它{RUBY_E#}为你揭示的，家的方向。」
t_en: "Your soul, your memories, the years you've journeyed through, and the path {RUBY_B#Time Titan, Oronyx}they{RUBY_E#} revealed, leading you back home."

t_ch: 「所以，前进吧。不要停下向前的脚步，不要辜负她的牺牲，还有曾经与你同行的一切——」
t_en: "So, continue onward. Do not stop moving forward, and do not let her sacrifice and the support of all who have stood by you be in vain..."

t_ch: 好伙伴，这边——！
t_en: Over here, friend...!

t_ch: 抓住人家的手，我们一起回家——！
t_en: Take my hand. Let's go home...!

t_ch: 再见了，冥界。
t_en: Goodbye, nether realm.

t_ch: 我来了，人间。
t_en: Hello again, mortal realm.

t_ch: 继续…「开拓」吧。
t_en: ...Let the Trailblazing... continue.

t_ch: （我…回来了。）
t_en: (I'm... back.)

t_ch: 终于…我知道，你能做到。
t_en: At last... I knew you could do it.

t_ch: 你的灵魂依旧是完满的，似乎有一种引力…在尽力将它维系。
t_en: Your soul is still perfectly whole, as if some kind of force is trying its best to keep it together.

t_ch: 是{RUBY_B#岁月之泰坦}欧洛尼斯{RUBY_E#}最后的祝福吗？还是…只属于你的奇迹呢？
t_en: Is it {RUBY_B#Time Titan}Oronyx{RUBY_E#}'s final blessing? Or... is it a miracle of your own?

t_ch: 真是…不可思议。冥冥之中，一种似曾相识的感觉涌上心头，难以言喻。
t_en: It's truly... incredible. Such a strange surge of familiarity... so hard to describe.

t_ch: 或许，是命运再度完成了自我实现；又或许，是{NICKNAME}阁下所熟知的、那天外世界的法则，令我们的{RUBY_B#命途}命运{RUBY_E#}早已交错……
t_en: Perhaps it is fate, once again fulfilling its own design. Or perhaps it is the laws from beyond the sky, so familiar to you, {NICKNAME}, that intertwined our {RUBY_B#Paths}fates{RUBY_E#} long ago...

t_ch: …看来，这不是眼下就能解开的谜团。
t_en: ...It seems that this is a riddle which cannot be solved anytime soon.

t_ch: 成为半神的感觉，很奇妙呢。仿佛冥河就在我灵魂深处流淌，于呼吸间化作温润世界的细雨……
t_en: The sensation of becoming a demigod is truly wondrous. It's as if the River of Souls is winding its way through the depths of my spirit, transforming with each breath into a fine rain that nourishes the world...

t_ch: 还记得那句预言吗？「花海尽头，生者的魂灵将温暖汝之指尖。相拥之后，便是永恒的离别。」
t_en: Do you recall that prophecy? "At the end of the sea of flowers, the souls of the living shall warm thy fingertips, and after an embrace... there shall be eternal separation."

t_ch: 我对阁下承诺过，会倾尽全力为它写下另一种诠释。现在，我既已成为半神，命运迎来结局——
t_en: I promised you that I would do everything in my power to give it another meaning. Now, as I stand as a demigod and destiny draws to a close...

t_ch: ——我也终于能够以最美丽的姿态，为它写下句点。
t_en: I can finally end it in a way that is as beautiful as it deserves.

t_ch: 那求而不得的心愿，终于能够实现。在崭新的世界里，每一朵绽放的生命，都将高举起双手，诉说这一刻的意义……
t_en: The wish I once sought but could never grasp is now within reach. In this brand-new world, every blooming life will lift its arms in celebration, and recount the profound meaning of this very instant...

t_ch: 我们所有人的每一次触碰，每一次拥抱，将再也不会是冰冷的剥夺……
t_en: Every touch, every embrace we share, will never again be an ice-cold loss...

t_ch: 而要带着炙热的体温，将「爱」播撒向大地。
t_en: They will radiate with an intense warmth, sowing love far and wide throughout the land.

t_ch: 现在 奥赫玛
t_en: Presently, in Okhema

t_ch: 方才那盛大又荒唐的表演，就是你的毕生所求？
t_en: That grand and absurd performance was what you've been seeking your whole life?

t_ch: 我为真理舍弃一切，就是为了换来今天这一刻。
t_en: I gave up everything in search of truth, and it culminated in this very moment.

t_ch: 倒是你诚如我所料，面对权力的可乘之机毫不犹豫。就像……
t_en: You, on the other hand, performed as I expected. You did not hesitate when you saw the chance to attain power. It's as if you...

t_ch: …嗜权如嗜腐的苍蝇。
t_en: ...Crave power like a fly craves rot.

t_ch: 我可没这么说。
t_en: Those are your words, not mine.

t_ch: 但最后的那番话…你已经知道了？我对白厄的安排。
t_en: But what you said at the end... Did you already know what I had planned for Phainon?

t_ch: 我失去了一只眼睛，视野却比任何人都更明朗。你人性将尽…与我这行尸走肉差不了多少了。
t_en: I may have lost an eye, but my vision is clearer than anyone else. You've nearly exhausted your humanity. You're no different from my current state as a walking corpse.

t_ch: 是啊，经过千年燃烧，我早已是一簇死灰。面对最后一尊大敌{RUBY_B#天空之泰坦}艾格勒{RUBY_E#}…也只有他能绽放与之匹敌的烈火。
t_en: That's true. I've long since burned to ash over the past one thousand years. Only he is capable of unleashing a raging fire that rivals our final great enemy — {RUBY_B#Sky Titan}Aquila{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 看来，这是最后的机会了…不妨让我们打破对彼此的芥蒂吧？
t_en: So, this is our last chance. How about we let go of the bad blood between us?

t_ch: 介意让我共鸣你的火种么，阿格莱雅？
t_en: May I resonate with your Coreflame, Aglaea?

t_ch: 请吧。
t_en: Please do.

t_ch: 这…怎么可能？仅凭如此稀薄的灵魂…你真是疯了。
t_en: That's... impossible. To persist with your soul thinned to such an extent... You're insane.

t_ch: 正如你能为未竟之事拖着死躯拼命前进，我也有不得不这么做的理由。
t_en: Just as you fight to lug your corpse onward to complete your unfinished goals, I have my own reasons for doing this.

t_ch: 在白厄成长为预言中的领袖之前，我必须维系逐火之旅，引领众人…哪怕只有一具空壳，只能前进寥寥几步。
t_en: Until Phainon grows into his position as the prophesied leader, I must continue to lead everyone on the Flame-Chase Journey. I must keep inching forward, even if only as an empty shell.

t_ch: 我早有预料，但没想到你已失却到这个地步……
t_en: I've long predicted this was the case, but I didn't think you'd already lost so much.

t_ch: 呵…何其讽刺。若你我能早些如这般交流，想必能免除不少误会吧。
t_en: Heh, how ironic. We would have avoided so many misunderstandings if you and I had just spoken this candidly before.

t_ch: 但抛开我们之间所有的理念差异，在这件事上，我始终与你想法一致——
t_en: But casting aside our ideological differences, there is one thing you and I agree on.

t_ch: 关于白厄。
t_en: Phainon.

t_ch: 正是。他是天生的英雄，洞悉人心的能力甚至远胜于你。我能断言：他比你更胜任领袖，也只有他能完成你们的使命……
t_en: Yes. He was born to be a hero. He understands the hearts of the people, and I can confidently say he is a far better leader than you. Only he is capable of fulfilling your mission.

t_ch: …可惜，他是个被{RUBY_B#浪漫之泰坦}墨涅塔{RUBY_E#}诅咒了的男人。不过根据我的猜想，他那穿红戴绿、花枝招展的可怕美学应该不耽误你们「再创世」。
t_en: ...Sadly, he is a man cursed by {RUBY_B#Romance Titan}Mnestia{RUBY_E#}. But by my estimates, his horribly elaborate sense of style should not impede Era Nova.

t_ch: 恭喜我们，在各自命运的末端达成了一样共识。
t_en: Congratulations are in order. We've found common ground at the very end of our destined paths.

t_ch: 你这般反应，甚至让我想收回前言了。
t_en: When you put it that way, it makes me want to take back what I said.

t_ch: 阿格莱雅！我们回来了——
t_en: Aglaea! We're back!

t_ch: 来欢迎我们浴火重生的大英雄，{NICKNAME}吧。
t_en: Come welcome {NICKNAME}, our great hero reborn from the ashes.

t_ch: 希望他没听到你刚才的评论。
t_en: I hope he didn't hear your earlier assessment.

t_ch: 哼，我这么批评他也不是第一天了——人贵有自知之明。
t_en: Hmph, it wouldn't be the first time I criticized him. A man should be aware of his own shortcomings.

t_ch: 不过…与诸位同行的人里，似乎没有遐蝶的身影。
t_en: But... I don't see Castorice among your number.

t_ch: 看来，她不仅为我完成了证明，也为你们完成了使命。
t_en: It looks like she not only helped me prove my theory, but helped you fulfill your mission as well.

t_ch: 她弄清了身世，也完成了试炼。
t_en: She finally understood her origins and completed her trial.

t_ch: 我相信她一直都在我们身边。
t_en: I believe she'll always be at our side.

t_ch: …我同意。
t_en: ...I agree.

t_ch: 虽无法改写宿命，却还是为自己的叙事诗写下了最后一枚韵脚……
t_en: She might not have been able to change her fate, but she still wrote the final verse of her epic herself.

t_ch: 吾师，请为我们吟诵神谕吧。{NICKNAME}，也请你为我们献上那枚滚烫的火种。
t_en: Trinnon, please recite the prophecy for us. {NICKNAME}, please surrender that scalding Coreflame for us as well.

t_ch: 你是她在大地上行走千年，亲手留住的唯一一条生命。我想…没有人比你更值得代行这高尚的职责。
t_en: You are the only life she ever saved by her own hand in her one thousand years on this earth. I believe no one is more qualified for this noble task.

t_ch: 那就让仪式开始吧。
t_en: Let the ceremony begin.

t_ch: 看呐，已回归的，或仍在远眺的众神！今天，我等将这剖白了的灵魂带来给你。
t_en: O! Gods who have returned, and gods who are still watching! Today, we bring you a soul laid bare.

t_ch: 灰黯之手，塞纳托斯，它已记起了昔日的名字。它从白昼中来，并在这里步入你们的行列，回到它芬芳的居室。
t_en: The Hand of Shadow, Thanatos, remembered their name from the past! From daylight they came and to their fragrant dwelling they return, to walk now among your ranks.

t_ch: 而那与泰坦携手的，是一位从死者中上升，在人间更生的旅人。{M#他}{F#她}是细数岁月的黑夜，凭{M#他}{F#她}正直的名字宣告——
t_en: And there was one who joined hands with the Titan. A traveler risen from the dead and reborn among mortal kind. {M#He}{F#She} is the everlasting night and shall now proclaim in {M#his}{F#her} good name:

t_ch: 「庄严的十二泰坦，支撑世界的支柱——」
t_en: "O majesty of the Twelve Titans, pillars of the world—"

t_ch: 「我们于此索求神性，以填补世界的裂缝——」
t_en: "We seek your divinity, to mend the rifts of the world —"

t_ch: 「为肉身灌注黄金之血，为神谕甘愿枯竭干涸……」
t_en: "Fill our bodies with blood of gold, till we wither in willing service to the prophecy..."

t_ch: 上前来，天外的开拓者，与*我们*一起——
t_en: Step forward, Trailblazer from beyond the sky. And together with us...

t_ch: 献上火种…为它在天空中刻下星辰吧。
t_en: Surrender the Coreflame and carve their star into the sky.

t_ch: 献上火种。
t_en: Surrender the Coreflame.

t_ch: 再会，遐蝶。
t_en: Goodbye, Castorice.

t_ch: 属于「死亡」的星座……
t_en: A constellation for Death...

t_ch: 准确地说…是「死亡」和「生命」。
t_en: For Life and Death, to be more precise.

t_ch: 你们看！那是……
t_en: Look! It's...

t_ch: 那是…遐蝶？
t_en: Is that... Castorice?

t_ch: 不，只是神性的回响…她是为了试炼而来？
t_en: No, it's only a Divine Echo. I think she's here for the trial.

t_ch: *泰坦的呢喃*
t_en: *Titan's murmurs*

t_ch: 她…说了什么？
t_en: What did she say?

t_ch: 那是…泰坦的语言……
t_en: That's the language of the Titans...

t_ch: 遐蝶…不，应该是泰坦……
t_en: Castorice... No, the Titan...

t_ch: 它说……
t_en: They said...

t_ch: 「…冰冷的死荫，已由我照亮。」
t_en: "...I have illuminated the cold shadow of death."

t_ch: 「走吧。前路将是光明，和永恒不熄的烈火。」
t_en: "Onwards. For your path to the future is bright and its flame eternal."

t_ch: 「别忘记……」
t_en: "Do not forget..."

t_ch: 呵，最后还把场面搞成这样…接下来轮到我了。
t_en: Heh, she made quite a scene. I suppose it's my turn now.

t_ch: …我不会阻止你。
t_en: I won't stop you...

t_ch: 老师，你……
t_en: Professor, you...

t_ch: 什么表情，你不是也在场么？
t_en: What's with that look? You were present.

t_ch: 好了，尽快完成处决吧——以免奥赫玛的神圣律法蒙羞，还耽误我创造新世界。
t_en: So, let's get this execution over with. We wouldn't want to bring shame to Okhema's divine laws or keep me from creating a new world.

t_ch: 老师…你永远都这样。
t_en: You never change, do you, Professor?

t_ch: 我不明白。如果你的理论不假，那成为泰坦的你应当没有前世的任何记忆…那成神又有什么意义？
t_en: I don't understand. If your theory is correct, you'll lose all memories of your past life once you become a Titan, so what's the point of becoming a god?

t_ch: 当然有。我就算真忘了自己是谁，也依旧聪明至极。
t_en: Of course there's a point. Even if I forget who I am, I'll still have my brilliant mind.

t_ch: 找回前世记忆？自然不在话下。况且，不还有你么？
t_en: It goes without saying I'll find a way to retrieve memories of my past life. Besides, I still have you, don't I?

t_ch: 你在大会上说的那番话，我还是没能消化。就算它是真的…按照记忆重塑而成的事物，也不能和原来的画等号了。
t_en: I still don't really understand what you said during the assembly. Even if it were true, things reforged from memory could never be the same as the original anyway.

t_ch: 那我现在就告诉你：你错了。
t_en: Then allow me to tell you right now that you're wrong.

t_ch: 「我们」究竟为何物？我说，黄金裔就是未来的泰坦，反之亦然，但这个答案显然只对我们有效。
t_en: What exactly are we? My answer is that the Chrysos Heirs are the future Titans, and vice versa. Of course, this answer only applies to us.

t_ch: 那一般人呢？对他们来说，「我们」又究竟为何物？——
t_en: So, what are "we" when it comes to regular mortals?

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>世界的本质——灵魂——就像一粒粒微不可见的种子，记录着某人对世界本身的部分记忆。而「我们」正是种子绽出的芽，长成的参天大树。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>The very essence of the world — our souls — are tiny invisible seeds that hold records of each person's memories of the world, and "we" are the buds that sprout from these seeds to grow into great trees.</color>

t_ch: 这…就是「智种」的含义？
t_en: Is that what Nousporism means?

t_ch: 不错。当我们的肉体诞生，便成为世界新的延伸。由此，我们的种子也在某人记忆中播下，并在未来播散到无数人心中。
t_en: Correct. Once our physical bodies are born, we become new extensions of the world. From there, we plant our seeds into someone's memories and then scatter them into the hearts of countless people in the future.

t_ch: 正如种子会受环境影响，我们的面貌也将在诸多记忆里有所不同，但我们承载的那一部分将永恒不变，因为世界本身正是如此。
t_en: Just as seeds are affected by their environment, so too will our appearances differ across memories. But the partial memories we carry will always be the same, because that is just how the world is.

t_ch: 要想消灭我们的存在，就得毁灭世界本身。然而，总有一人将带着他完美的记忆活下去……
t_en: To destroy our very existence, one must destroy the world itself. But we have someone who will still live on with his perfect memory...

t_ch: 那个人就是你，刻法勒之子——这也便是「负世之泰坦」的含义。
t_en: That person is you, child of Kephale. This is what it means to be the Worldbearing Titan.

t_ch: 所以，背负这个世界的全部，活下去。别让你珍重之人、别让「金织」女士失望——
t_en: So, carry everything of this world and live on. Don't disappoint those most precious to you. Don't disappoint Lady Goldweaver.

t_ch: ——更别让我的理论蒙羞。
t_en: And especially don't bring dishonor to my theory.

t_ch: 吾师，我向你发誓：我会引领所有人在新世界重逢。
t_en: Anaxa, I swear to you. I'll lead everyone to our reunion in the new world.

t_ch: 哼，很好。
t_en: Hmph, good.

t_ch: 言尽于此。别了，各位，来世再会。
t_en: That's all I have to say. Farewell, everyone. May we meet again in the next life.

t_ch: 别了，「大表演家」。愿瑟希斯捍卫你的思想。
t_en: Farewell, Great Performer. May Cerces safeguard your thoughts.

t_ch: 以同样的方式致敬。
t_en: Salute him in the same way.

t_ch: 愿瑟希斯捍卫你的思想。
t_en: May Cerces safeguard your thoughts.

t_ch: 可还算满意，尊贵的泰坦？
t_en: Are you satisfied, honorable Titan?

t_ch: 当然。吾在此立誓见证：神悟树庭「智种学派」的阿那克萨戈拉斯，业已战胜理性之试炼。
t_en: Very. I hereby swear I have borne witness: Anaxagoras of the Nousporists and the Grove of Epiphany has successfully overcome the trial of Reason.

t_ch: 于是，吾亦赐汝箴言——
t_en: Thus, I shall confer upon you these words.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>「汝将超越至纯粹之终极，回归腐败苦黑。」</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>"You shall transcend the peaks of purity and return to corruption and hardship."</color>

t_ch: 呵，这就是所谓神谕？听起来只是把我过去、现在和未来成就的一切复述了一遍。
t_en: Oh, so that's a god's prophecy? It sounds like you just rephrased my past, present, and future.

t_ch: 瑟希斯啊瑟希斯，你确实不过如此。
t_en: Oh, Cerces, Cerces. Is that all you've got?

t_ch: 啊呀…毕竟汝已「超越至纯粹之终极」，可曾为吾留下哪怕一点余地哪？
t_en: Well, you've already "transcended the peaks of purity" and you were never the type to give me any leeway.

t_ch: 哼，承让。
t_en: Hmph, that is true.

t_ch: 不过，吾还是有个问题……
t_en: But I do still have one question.

t_ch: 我就知道……
t_en: I knew it...

t_ch: 说，什么问题？我们搞不好在想同一件事。
t_en: What is it? Maybe we're thinking of the same thing.

t_ch: 既然汝斗胆声明，吾等连同世界本身，皆凭他人记忆而生……
t_en: As you've so boldly stated, we and the world itself live on through the memories of others.

t_ch: 那末…最初的智种，又要在谁人的记忆中生根发芽呢？
t_en: If that is the case, whose memories did the very first Nouspore sprout from?

t_ch: 我怎么知道？
t_en: How should I know?

t_ch: 啊呀呀……
t_en: Oh my...

t_ch: 不过，既然你我都对此感到好奇——
t_en: But since we are both equally curious...

t_ch: ——那就让我们身后的诸位人子，亲自代你我一探究竟吧。
t_en: We should allow the children of humanity who come after us to investigate in our stead.

t_ch: 阁下，求你，为我回头吧。
t_en: Please, I beg you, look back for me.

t_ch: 死亡是多么甜蜜…
t_en: Death is such a sweet joy...

t_ch: 来一场拥抱吧…
t_en: A warm embrace awaits you...

t_ch: 别再经历人间的苦难。
t_en: Just forget about all the hardships of the mortal realm.

t_ch: 别走，阁下。
t_en: Don't go.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，这边这边！
t_en: {NICKNAME}, over here!

t_ch: 向前走，别回头。
t_en: Keep moving forward and don't look back.

t_ch: 前进吧，列车会和你一起。
t_en: Keep going. The Express is right here with you.

t_ch: 我相信你，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: I believe in you, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 听我说：向前。
t_en: Listen: just keep moving.

t_ch: 总有一天，我们的道路还会相交。
t_en: One day, our paths will cross again.

t_ch: 没错，向着黎明升起的方向。
t_en: That's right. Keep your eyes on the dawn.

t_ch: 昂首挺胸，和*我们*一起走向明天吧！
t_en: Lift your chin up high and step forward with us toward tomorrow!

t_ch: 前进！让我看见你的勇气。
t_en: Onward! Show me your courage.

t_ch: 搭档，与我一同——成为英雄吧。
t_en: Partner, we shall become heroes together!

t_ch: （快到了……）
t_en: (Almost there...)

t_ch: 我衷心为这场凯旋感到欣喜……
t_en: I'm gladdened by your triumphant return...

t_ch: …该庆幸的是，她还活着。
t_en: ...Thankfully, she's still alive.

t_ch: 列车最初打捞起三月七时，她也是这样？
t_en: Was March 7th also in this condition when she was first rescued by the Express?

t_ch: 情况很相似。只是那会儿，列车上没有一位忆者能替她诊断。
t_en: Her condition was very similar. It's just that at that time, the Express did not have a Memokeeper who could diagnose her condition.

t_ch: 可她还是自然苏醒了。乐观点想，或许这次也有希望。
t_en: But she still woke up naturally. Looking on the bright side, that might happen this time as well.

t_ch: 从冰封中醒来时，三月的记忆一片空白。做最坏的打算，我决不能让同样的事发生第二次。
t_en: When March awakened from the ice, her memories were completely wiped clean. I want to prepare for the worst and prevent something like that from happening again.

t_ch: 还是来说说你的猜想吧？
t_en: Can you share with us your hypothesis?

t_ch: 虽然证据还不充分，但依现状推断……
t_en: I do not have conclusive evidence, but judging by her current condition...

t_ch: 三月七，<color=#dbc291ff>她的记忆或许被人劫持了。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Perhaps March 7th's memories have been hijacked.</color>

t_ch: 劫持？
t_en: Hijacked?

t_ch: 姬子小姐，试想一下，如果一个人失去了「记忆的能力」会怎样？
t_en: Think about it, Miss Himeko. What will happen when someone loses their ability to remember?

t_ch: 我并非生物学家。听说翁瓦克有一种鱼类，记忆容量不足一秒，过目即忘。也许会发生类似的事？
t_en: I'm not a biologist, but I hear that there's of a species of fish on Vonwacq that has the memory span of less than a second. It forgets anything that occurs in the very next instant. Perhaps something similar will happen?

t_ch: 很可惜，「短时记忆」和「无法记忆」是完全不同的概念。或许你难以想象……
t_en: Unfortunately, short-term memory is an entirely different concept from the inability to remember. Perhaps it is difficult for you to imagine...

t_ch: 失去记忆机能的个体，会变成一片空无。
t_en: But an individual who has lost their ability to remember turns into nothingness.

t_ch: 空无？
t_en: Nothingness?

t_ch: 寰宇间的一切都能以记忆诠释，这是忆庭的信条，也是这条命途存续的动力。
t_en: The belief of the Garden is that everything in this universe can be interpreted through memories. This is the driving force behind the continuance of this Path.

t_ch: 「过往」由忆质构成，「未来」也是终将被转化的可能性，而「当下」…它从不真实存在，只是一种抽象的表达。
t_en: The past is made up of memoria while the future is a possibility that will eventually be converted to memoria. But the present... it doesn't really exist and is only an abstract expression.

t_ch: 所以，当一个人受命途影响，丧失了记忆的机能…他的实体也会受到牵连。这些冰晶，就是表征之一。
t_en: Therefore, when a person is affected by the Path and loses their ability to remember... their physical form will also be affected. These ice crystals are a manifestation of that.

t_ch: …有件事我始终没有深究，黑天鹅小姐。
t_en: ...Miss Black Swan, there is one thing that I haven't looked into deeply until now.

t_ch: 你引领我们前来翁法罗斯，却始终不愿谈及真实目的。「收集记忆」…并不是一个能让我信服的回答。
t_en: You've guided us to Amphoreus but refused to tell us your true intentions. "Collecting memories"... is simply not a convincing answer.

t_ch: 姬子小姐是在怀疑我么？
t_en: Is Miss Himeko suspicious of me?

t_ch: 无意冒犯，我只是提出一种猜想。但如果可以，我希望能得到你的坦诚。
t_en: I mean no offense. I am merely speculating. But I'd appreciate your honesty, if possible.

t_ch: 也就是说，到我自证清白的时间了……
t_en: In other words, now is the time for me to prove my innocence...

t_ch: 当然，没问题。真正的原因是：翁法罗斯有着特殊的意义，在一些秉持极端理念的忆庭成员眼中，它…是一个「完美的范例」。
t_en: That's not a problem, of course. The true reason behind my intentions is that Amphoreus holds special significance. For some Memokeepers of the Garden with extremist views, it... is a "perfect example."

t_ch: 完美的范例？
t_en: Perfect example?

t_ch: 一个只能被忆庭之镜映照出的世界，意味着忆者拥有将其「私藏」的权利。如果那些「记忆」行者先一步掌握了翁法罗斯的奥秘……
t_en: A world that can only be seen through the Garden's mirror means that Memokeepers have the right to keep the world in their private collection. If those pathstriders of Remembrance were to uncover the secrets of Amphoreus first...

t_ch: 他们也许会释放野心，将更多世界改造成相同的模样。
t_en: They might unleash their wild ambitions and remodel more worlds into a similar form.

t_ch: 你尽力游说列车来到这片星域，为的是揭开秘密，挫败这些人的阴谋？
t_en: So, the reason you did everything you could to convince the Express to come to this star zone is just to uncover the secrets and foil the plans of these people?

t_ch: 此前我闭口不提，是不想将各位卷入忆庭内部的纷争。
t_en: I chose to remain quiet before because I did not want to involve you in the Garden's internal conflicts.

t_ch: 然而，改造世界绝非易事。那些极端分子想必不是普通的命途行者，他们拥有令使的力量？
t_en: Remodeling a world is no easy feat. These extremists likely aren't just simple pathstriders. Do they wield the powers of Emanators?

t_ch: 不妨再大胆些，我口中的人…并非个例，而是一整个「组织」。他们早已牵上了浮黎的小指。
t_en: You can be even bolder with your guesses. The people that I'm talking about... They aren't just individuals. They are an organization that has already attached itself to the pinky finger of Fuli.

t_ch: 挟持三月七的人，会是他们么？
t_en: So what you're saying... Could they be the ones who hijacked March 7th's memories?

t_ch: 我不敢肯定。至少，他们能做到。
t_en: I cannot be certain, but at the very least, they are capable of doing so.

t_ch: 我对三月七的遭遇深表遗憾，但我提供的交易依旧公平。翁法罗斯仍是一处绝佳的目的地，继续调查，或许也能探明意外的真相。
t_en: I am deeply sorry about what has happened to March 7th, but the deal that I proposed still remains a fair one. Amphoreus is still an excellent destination, and investigating further might allow us to uncover the truth behind the accident.

t_ch: 姬子小姐，继续合作才是最明智的选择……
t_en: Miss Himeko, the wisest choice right now is for us to continue cooperating...

t_ch: 发生什么了？
t_en: What's wrong?

t_ch: …我部分收回刚才的话，姬子小姐。
t_en: ...Let me take back a part of what I said just now, Miss Himeko.

t_ch: 我们确实做了一桩交易，但我也让你们卷入了不可预见的危机中…请接受我的致歉。
t_en: Although we indeed struck a deal, it seems like I have involved you in some unforeseen danger... Please accept my deepest apologies.

t_ch: 情况…有变？
t_en: Has something changed?

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>浮黎…祂的目光，刚刚掠过了翁法罗斯。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>It's Fuli... THEIR gaze has just swept across Amphoreus.</color>

t_ch: …哦？这倒有点意思。
t_en: ...Oh? How interesting.

t_ch: 没想到小小一个窃忆者，脑袋里还藏了不少东西。
t_en: I never expected a mere Memosnatcher to be actually hiding so much inside her head.

t_ch: 更有趣的是，她的潜意识里也出现了「翁法罗斯」这四个字。
t_en: What's even more interesting is that the word "Amphoreus" has appeared in her subconscious.

t_ch: 这位女士…似乎乐在其中。她经常这样吗？
t_en: This lady... seems to be having fun. Is she usually like this?

t_ch: …三言两语难以言明。
t_en: ...It's hard to explain.

t_ch: 在说什么悄悄话呢？有没有想过，我听得一清二楚？
t_en: What are you two whispering about? Has it not occurred to you that I can hear everything clearly?

t_ch: 失礼了，黑塔女士，介意分享您的发现吗？
t_en: Please pardon me, Madam Herta. Would you mind sharing with us what you've discovered?

t_ch: 哼，有求于人的是星穹列车。就算手段不那么常规，你们也得忍着。
t_en: Hmph, the ones who need help are the Astral Express. You will have to put up with my methods, even if they are rather unconventional.

t_ch: 言归正传，我借着第四面镜，在那窃忆者的脑子里翻了个底朝天，收获颇丰。
t_en: Let's get back to the topic. By using the Fourth Mirror, I rummaged through the brain of this Memosnatcher and learned a good deal.

t_ch: 「翁法罗斯」——不仅这人知道它，还有很多忆者都对那里感兴趣。
t_en: Not only does this person know of Amphoreus, but there are also many Memokeepers who are interested in the place.

t_ch: 我试着在她的意识库里检索，然后找到了这些词：「永恒之地」、「拒绝之地」、「忆庭的私藏」……
t_en: I searched through her consciousness and found the following terms: "The Eternal Land," "The Rejected Land," and "the Private Collection of the Garden of Recollection"...

t_ch: 看来，黑天鹅小姐的确有所隐瞒。
t_en: It seems like Miss Black Swan is indeed hiding some things from us.

t_ch: 这个神秘兮兮的翁法罗斯，既然从外部找不到有用的线索，那我干脆——登门拜访。
t_en: Since I'm not able to find any useful clues from the outside, why don't I personally visit this mysterious Amphoreus?

t_ch: 您要亲自前往翁法罗斯？
t_en: You're planning to head to Amphoreus yourself?

t_ch: 对——但不是像你们那样傻乎乎地靠肉身闯关。
t_en: Yes, but not in the flesh, like you all foolishly did.

t_ch: 看见了吗？黑塔魔镜，靠它们几个，我足不出户就能完成空间折跃，将自己的「数据精神体」投射到银河各个角落。
t_en: See these Herta Mirrors? With them, I can project my "data spirit entity" to any corner of the cosmos through spatial warps, without even needing to leave my room.

t_ch: 当然，前提是那些「角落」在星际和平通信的服务范围内。这是一项联名技术。
t_en: But of course, these "corners of the cosmos" must be within the coverage zone of Interastral Peace Communications. After all, this is collaborative technology.

t_ch: 原来如此。您的气质确实更加…「室内派」。
t_en: I see. You do seem like the more... "indoor" type.

t_ch: 虽然那片星域不在公司网络覆盖下，但列车本身也能充当一个信号基站。来，给我一个星图坐标。
t_en: Although that star zone is not within the network of the IPC, the Express itself can serve as a signal station. Come, give me the astral chart coordinates.

t_ch: 然后，就等着看好戏吧。
t_en: Then, sit back and enjoy the show.

t_ch: 瓦尔特将星穹列车的坐标分享给了大黑塔……
t_en: Welt shares the Astral Express's coordinates with The Herta...

t_ch: 第一面镜，把坐标输进去，你来负责空间折跃。
t_en: First Mirror, input the coordinates. You'll be in charge of the spatial warp.

t_ch: 遵命，黑塔女士。
t_en: As you command, Madam Herta.

t_ch: 准备工作已完成，祝您路途平安。
t_en: The preparations are complete. I wish you a pleasant journey ahead.

t_ch: 第二面镜，准备「思维折射」。从星穹列车到翁法罗斯天体还有一段距离，你得把我发射过去——
t_en: Second Mirror, prepare for the Thought Refraction. There's still some distance from the Astral Express to the Amphoreus celestial body. You're in charge of sending me over...

t_ch: ——这次记得把功率拉满，别抠抠搜搜的！
t_en: This time raise the power to the maximum. Don't be stingy!

t_ch: 好、好的，一定不负使命，黑塔女士……
t_en: O—Okay, I won't fail the mission, Madam Herta...

t_ch: 第三面镜，你来负责生成「数据精神体」吧。
t_en: Third Mirror, you're in charge of generating the "data spirit entity."

t_ch: 欸嘿嘿…乐意效劳，黑塔女士……
t_en: Hehehe... I'm happy to help, Madam Herta...

t_ch: 这一次…嘿嘿嘿…您打算用个什么形象呐？
t_en: Hehehe... What kind of appearance are you planning to use this time?

t_ch: 好问题，我想想……
t_en: Good question. Let me think...

t_ch: 算了，就用我本人的样子吧。反正那里也没熟人。
t_en: Forget it. Just use my own appearance. There's no one there that'll recognize me anyway.

t_ch: 嘻嘻嘻，都听您的……
t_en: Hehehe, as you wish...

t_ch: 第四面镜，你负责……
t_en: Fourth Mirror, you'll be in charge of...

t_ch: 好像用不着你了。原地待命吧。
t_en: It seems like I have nothing for you. Just stand by and wait for orders.

t_ch: 啊？又让我待命？太无聊啦……
t_en: Ah? Standing by again? That's so boring...

t_ch: 要不，下次您从四往下数…？
t_en: Next time, can you count down from four instead...?

t_ch: 有劳了，黑塔女士。「伙伴」是星穹列车的命脉。此事过后，我们一定会对您伸出的援手予以报偿。
t_en: Thank you, Madam Herta. Companions are the lifeblood of the Astral Express. When things are settled, we will repay you for all your help.

t_ch: （瓦尔特…这年轻人还真有礼貌啊。）
t_en: (Welt... This young man sure is courteous.)

t_ch: 观看您的工作过程令我深受启发，女士。我很难想象，一位像您这样卓越的学者，平日的状态竟如此…松弛。
t_en: Madam, watching you work has been an enlightening experience for me. It's hard to imagine that an outstanding scholar like you could maintain such a... relaxed manner most of the time.

t_ch: 您表现出的从容不迫，正是我应当学习的特质。
t_en: Your calm and collected nature is truly a trait that I should learn.

t_ch: （这小子…怎么感觉他每句话都在阴阳怪气？）
t_en: (This brat... Why does everything that comes out of his mouth sound so snarky?)

t_ch: 数据精神体准备完了。
t_en: Preparation of the data spirit entity, complete.

t_ch: 思维折射，功率已经拉到最大。
t_en: Thought Refraction, at max power.

t_ch: 很好——第一面镜，我们出发吧。
t_en: Excellent — First Mirror, let's get going.

t_ch: 旅途平安，黑塔女士。
t_en: Have a safe trip, Madam Herta.

t_ch: 希望您有所收获。
t_en: May your journey yield good results.

t_ch: …坐标输入完毕，准备启动空间折跃。目标：未知天体「翁法罗斯」。
t_en: ...Coordinates input complete. Preparing to activate spatial warp. Destination: Unknown celestial body, Amphoreus.

t_ch: 空间折跃倒计时：五，四，三，二，一……
t_en: Spatial warp countdown: Five, four, three, two, one...

t_ch: 折跃，启动。
t_en: Warp activated.

t_ch: 嚯…那就是翁法罗斯？外形还真是别具一格。
t_en: Oh... So that's Amphoreus? Its appearance is — well — sure is unique.

t_ch: 第二面镜，准备「思维折射」——送我进去吧！
t_en: Second Mirror, prepare for Thought Refraction — it's time to send me in!

t_ch: 好——请、请您仔细阅读安全协议……
t_en: Alright, p—please read the safety protocols carefully...

t_ch: 能不能麻利点？
t_en: Can't you hurry up?

t_ch: 遵、遵命！
t_en: Y—Yes, ma'am!

t_ch: 哎哟…没想到这么颠簸。早知道就让第二面镜把功率调低点了。
t_en: Agh... I didn't expect it to be so turbulent. I should have asked the Second Mirror to use slightly less power.

t_ch: 嚯…好一番壮观的景象，这就是翁法罗斯的入口吗？
t_en: Oh... What a magnificent sight. Is this the entrance to Amphoreus?

t_ch: 让我看看，指不定能找到什么门路呢？
t_en: Let's see. Perhaps I'll be able to find a way in?

t_ch: 这是什么，雕像？难看，不喜欢。
t_en: What's this? A statue? It's ugly. I don't like it.

t_ch: 喔，找到你们…的影子了。
t_en: Oh, I've finally found your... traces.

t_ch: 什么人能让黑塔亲自出山？准备好，你俩这辈子都要当模拟宇宙的免费劳动力了。
t_en: How esteemed must you be for the great Herta to come out personally! When we return, get ready to become free labor for the Simulated Universe for the rest of your lives.

t_ch: 这是什么？看着怪阴森的。
t_en: What's this? How creepy.

t_ch: …哦，还会动？吓唬谁呢。
t_en: ...Oh, it moves? Who are you trying to scare?

t_ch: 不是投影就是假人，似乎也没什么可留意的。
t_en: It's either a projection or a fake. There doesn't seem to be much to take note of.

t_ch: 接下来…该怎么进入翁法罗斯内部呢？
t_en: Now then... How am I going to get into Amphoreus?

t_ch: 向您致歉，尊贵的女士。您恐怕不能继续前进了。
t_en: My deepest apologies, esteemed lady. I'm afraid that you cannot proceed further.

t_ch: 谁？
t_en: Who's there?

t_ch: 您的莅临在我计算之外，却也带来了惊喜。可惜未能做足准备，以符合您身份的礼节周到款待。
t_en: Your visit, although not part of my calculations, is a pleasant surprise. It's a pity that the preparations are not yet in place to receive you with the hospitality that someone of your status deserves.

t_ch: 不打算回答我的问题吗？你是谁？
t_en: Are you not going to answer my question? Who are you?

t_ch: 身份，我有过许多，未来还会拥有更多。但若您只是需要一个称呼，不妨用我惯常的化名吧——
t_en: I have many identities, and I'm going to have even more in the future. But if you just need a form of address, you can use my common alias...

t_ch: 吕枯耳戈斯，来古士。
t_en: Lycurgus, Lygus.

t_ch: 好啊，来古士——你不会觉得，我想问的是一个名字吧？
t_en: Alright, Lygus. But you can't possibly think that I'm just asking for a name, can you?

t_ch: 很遗憾，我并无权限为您说明更多。
t_en: Regrettably, I do not have the authority to share more information.

t_ch: 但标准总是灵活的。在不违反终极协议的前提下，我可以透露一条信息：如您所想，此地是「翁法罗斯」的入口。
t_en: However, standards are always flexible. While not violating the ultimate protocol, I'm able to disclose this piece of information: As you thought, this is the entrance to Amphoreus.

t_ch: 而现在——请回吧，女士。很遗憾，翁法罗斯不是您应当染指的世界。无论您建立过多少丰功伟绩，此地都不会轻易敞开大门。
t_en: Now, please kindly turn back. Amphoreus is not a world that you should be involved with. Regardless of what kind of magnificent achievements you have accomplished, this place will not open its doors so easily.

t_ch: 你似乎很了解我啊。那你应当知道，我凭什么要听你的？
t_en: You seem to understand me very well. Then you should know that I have no reason to listen to you.

t_ch: 您的反应正如我的计算。看来，即便经历了漫长的守望，我对人性的测算仍旧准确。在您身上，存在着美丽的求知欲。
t_en: Your reaction is completely within my calculations. Despite my long years as a watcher, it seems like my assessment of human nature still remains accurate. From you, I am able to sense a beautiful desire for knowledge.

t_ch: 但我的回答不会改变。女士，我以最大的善意建议您原路返回。
t_en: But my answer remains unchanged. Madam, it is with the utmost goodwill that I suggest you return to where you came from.

t_ch: 假设一下，来古士先生——如果我没有这么好的脾气，如果我非要瞧瞧你背后的秘密——
t_en: Let's assume for a second that I'm not so well-mannered and that I insist on uncovering the secrets hidden behind you, Mr. Lygus...

t_ch: ——你打算怎么消解我这任性的念头？
t_en: ...How do you plan to dispel this notion of mine?

t_ch: 在终极协议划定的范围内，我有<unbreak>178244</unbreak>条可被用于说服您的逻辑链。
t_en: Within the scope of the ultimate protocol, there are <unbreak>178,244</unbreak> logic chains at my disposal to convince you.

t_ch: 而经过筛选后，我认为只需其中3条便能达成我想要的结果。
t_en: After filtering through them, I have determined that I only require three to attain the results that I desire.

t_ch: 哦？说来听听吧。
t_en: Oh? I'm all ears.

t_ch: 其一：由于不可言说的原因，翁法罗斯是「绝对封闭」的天体。除我以外，没有任何能协助您进入其中的方法。
t_en: First: For reasons that cannot be disclosed, Amphoreus is a completely isolated celestial body. Apart from me, no one else will be able to help you enter.

t_ch: 这可算不上什么理由。
t_en: That's not a good argument.

t_ch: 其二：若您计划——正如您刚才暗示的那般——以宣战为由胁迫我与您合作，还请知晓……
t_en: Second: If your plan is — as you've alluded to previously — to force me into cooperating by showing hostility, do know that...

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>我和您一样，也曾沐浴「智识」的瞥视，</color>并以尊严向您保证，在极端情境下，我们的毁灭互有保证。
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Just like you, I have basked in the gaze of the Erudition.</color> I stake my honor and assure you that in the worst-case scenario, our mutual destruction will be inevitable.

t_ch: 怪不得我扫描了一圈都没找着后门。你身上的安全协议…倒是和螺丝不相上下，有意思。
t_en: No wonder I couldn't find a backdoor when I did a scan of our surroundings. The safety protocols on you... are comparable to Screwy's. How interesting.

t_ch: 想必，您还想听听第三条逻辑链？
t_en: I assume that you still wish to hear the third logic chain?

t_ch: 当然，为什么不呢？
t_en: Of course. Why wouldn't I?

t_ch: 其三：即便您能用尽各种手段，试图找到突破我的办法……
t_en: Third: Even if you exhaust all your means and find a way to break through...

t_ch: 但在得知此举必将导致的后果后，您也会放弃尝试。毕竟，在您漫长的人生中——即便您可能不会承认——「良知」扮演了极为重要的角色。
t_en: You will surely give up once you understand the consequences of your actions. After all, throughout your long life — as much as you'd hate to admit — your conscience has played a crucial role.

t_ch: 哈哈！好个大言不惭的机器人。你觉得只凭自己那点计算，就谈得上了解我了？
t_en: Haha! What a brazen robot. You think that you can actually understand me through your paltry calculations?

t_ch: 但话都说到这个份上了，不妨讲讲看吧：那后果是什么？
t_en: But since you've put it that way, why don't you tell me what these consequences will be?

t_ch: 它关乎缠绕翁法罗斯的第三条命途，也是您和您的朋友至今未能解明的那一条……
t_en: It concerns the third Path that is entangled with Amphoreus — a Path that you and your friends still very much lack understanding of...

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>「毁灭」。</color>若您无视我的警告，一意孤行——
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>The Destruction.</color> So, if you ignore my warnings and persist in your ways...

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>您将打碎一位「绝灭大君」的枷锁，而它的愤怒将吞没银河。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>You will break the shackles of a Lord Ravager, and when that happens, their fury will engulf the cosmos.</color>

t_ch: 正在众人静候「纷争」的半神从试炼中诞生时，意外也发生了。开拓小队立即随万敌深入试炼，并救出了困于其中的白厄。正当众人松了一口气时，新的问题却随之而来。
t_en: While everyone is awaiting the demigod of Strife to emerge from the trial, something unexpected happens. The Trailblaze squad immediately follows Mydei into the trial and rescues Phainon, who is trapped inside. Just as everyone breathes a sigh of relief, a new problem arises.

t_ch: 万敌不得不肩负起「纷争」的重任，但悬锋人也绝不会将火种拱手让给宿敌奥赫玛。为了寻求理解，他决定首先向恩师，悬锋老将克拉特鲁斯请教，二人不欢而散。
t_en: Mydei had to shoulder the burden of Strife, but the Kremnoans would never willingly give up the Coreflame to their old foe Okhema. In an attempt to gain a better understanding, he decides to first seek advice from his teacher Krateros, a veteran of Kremnos. The two part on bad terms.

t_ch: 与此同时，奥赫玛向神悟树庭派出以缇安、遐蝶为代表的外交使节。众人抵达时意外发现黑潮来袭，并与神秘剑士交战，但最终还是成功救出那刻夏和他身负的火种。
t_en: Meanwhile, Trianne and Castorice are dispatched to the Grove of Epiphany as diplomatic envoys of Okhema. When they arrive, they discover to their surprise that the black tide has struck, and they fight a mysterious swordmaster. In the end, they manage to rescue Anaxa and the Coreflame that he carries.

t_ch: 就在众人返程途中，奥赫玛城内，悬锋老将因挟持缇宁，擅闯禁地被捕。在万敌斡旋下，缇宝等人同意袒露记忆，以便实现悬锋人与奥赫玛人的和解。
t_en: While everyone is on their way back, within the city of Okhema, Krateros is arrested for abducting Trinnon and trespassing on forbidden grounds. Under Mydei's mediation, Tribbie and her sisters agree to reveal their memories in order to facilitate reconciliation between the Kremnoans and Okhemans.

t_ch: 不料，就在缇宝等人打算谒见「岁月」泰坦欧洛尼斯，修补记忆时，意外再次遭遇神秘剑士，缇安为保护同伴与泰坦一道陨落。为了夺回火种，众人制定了「封印」计划。
t_en: Unexpectedly, just as Tribbie and her sisters are about to meet the Time Titan Oronyx and repair their memories, they encounter the mysterious swordmaster yet again. Trianne sacrifices herself and falls with the Titan to protect her companions. In order to take back the Coreflame, everyone set up a plan to "seal" the swordmaster.

t_ch: 计划一波三折，但万敌以「纷争」半神之姿归来，协助众人成功夺回火种。不久后，他成功说服子民放下对宿仇的成见，便与众人告别，返回家乡，踏上对抗黑潮的前线。
t_en: The plan suffers multiple setbacks, but Mydei returns as the demigod of Strife and helps everyone reclaim the Coreflame. Not long after, he successfully persuades his people to let go of their prejudices against Okhema. He says goodbye to everyone and returns home to join the frontline against the black tide.

t_ch: 另一边，众人为神力耗尽、化作娃娃的缇安举行了简单的葬礼。接过缇安的遗愿，众人将在逐火之旅的道路上继续向前……
t_en: On the other side, everyone holds a simple funeral for Trianne, who exhausted her divine powers and turned into a doll. Having accepted Trianne's last wish, the group continues on the path of the Flame-Chase Journey...

t_ch: 于十五个{RUBY_B#黎明}门扉时{RUBY_E#}到来前…去夺回你的「未来」吧……
t_en: Before the passing of fifteen {RUBY_B#Dawn}Entry Hours{RUBY_E#}... Go forth and reclaim your future...

t_ch: 这便是「岁月」真正的试炼……
t_en: This is the true trial of "time"...

t_ch: 很好，既然万事俱备……
t_en: Alright, now that everything is ready...

t_ch: 镜子们，轮到你们出场了。
t_en: Mirrors, get to work.
Tsk...

t_ch: 既已至此，还是稍安勿躁罢。
t_en: Since we've come this far, let's just wait.

t_ch: 格奈乌斯阁下，以及吾师，你们也是来这里问候卡厄斯的吗？
t_en: Lord Gnaeus and Miss Calypso? Are you both here to visit Khaos too?

t_ch: 正是，吾等已在此地等待有些时间了……
t_en: Yes, and we've been waiting for quite some time now...

t_ch: 可惜，他还是没有回应。
t_en: Regrettably, he hasn't answered us.

t_ch: 最坏的情况：「负世」的重量…或许已将他压垮。
t_en: The worst-case scenario is that he's... already been overwhelmed and crushed by the Worldbearing burden.

t_ch: 格奈乌斯阁下，你多虑了。我能感觉到，他的魂息仍在此地盘桓。
t_en: You're overthinking, Lord Gnaeus. I can still sense Khaos' soul in the area.

t_ch: 况且，你我都明白卡厄斯的为人：身为救世主，他绝不会如此轻易倒下，更不会抛下他记念的一切，不是么？
t_en: And besides, we both know Khaos' nature. A Deliverer like him wouldn't fall so readily, much less give up on all that he holds dear, isn't that so?

t_ch: 哎…我相信他。
t_en: Yes, I have faith in him.

t_ch: 话说回来，小玻吕茜亚，此行怎只有汝独自前来？
t_en: That reminds me. Why are you making this trip alone, Polyxia?

t_ch: 啊，姐姐说，她还需要些时间照看花朵……
t_en: Oh, my sister needs more time to make sure the flowers are fine...
Heh...

t_ch: 可怜见…汝二人生来便为接过{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}的权柄，却比任何人都心怀恻隐哪。
t_en: Such a pity... Both of you have hearts kinder than anyone else, yet you are born only to take over {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#}'s divinity.

t_ch: 这一次，我所听到的一切，我所见到的一切，全都令我深感震撼——
t_en: Everything that I see and hear this time has left me shaken to the core.

t_ch: 这场看似平常，实则空前绝后的交谈，已经足以为我揭晓{RUBY_B#「我们」究竟为何物}瑟希斯之问{RUBY_E#}的终极答案。
t_en: What may seem like a mundane conversation is, in truth, a once-in-a-lifetime exchange that has revealed the ultimate answer to {RUBY_B#What exactly are we?}Cerces' question{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 猜想已经形成，亟需落成定理，接下来只要向{RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}求证即可。如此一来，我便能够解答{RUBY_B#理性之泰坦}瑟希斯{RUBY_E#}的疑惑，夺回身体……
t_en: The conjecture has taken shape and must now be solidified into a theorem. Next, I only need to seek verification from {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#}. By doing so, I can answer {RUBY_B#Reason Titan}Cerces{RUBY_E#}' question and reclaim my body...

t_ch: …但我不会这么做。这不重要。
t_en: ...But no, I will not do that. That isn't important.

t_ch: 因为，我梦寐以求的另一道猜想，也在此时此刻证明完毕——
t_en: Because another one of my conjectures — one that I have always dreamed of proving — has been proven right this very instant.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>由凡人熔合泰坦的灵魂，取而代之…并非天方夜谭。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Fusing a mortal's soul with that of a Titan to replace said Titan... is not a ludicrous idea after all.</color>

t_ch: …汝真是位严师哪。
t_en: You truly are a strict teacher.

t_ch: 当然，我向来如此。
t_en: Of course. This is how I've always been.

t_ch: 阿那克萨戈拉斯阁下，久等了。
t_en: Sorry to keep you waiting, Lord Anaxagoras.

t_ch: 她已恭候多时，随我来吧。
t_en: She's been waiting. Follow me, please.

t_ch: 那刻夏老师——
t_en: Professor Anaxa...

t_ch: 这边——
t_en: Over here...

t_ch: 哼，渎神的学者也能来到这里？
t_en: Hmph, even blasphemous scholars can come here, it seems?

t_ch: 看来我在这里不受欢迎呀。
t_en: Looks like my presence here is quite unwelcome.

t_ch: 啊呀！那壁画上的大树…莫非是吾？
t_en: Oh my! Could it be that the Great Tree painted in this mural... is actually depicting me?

t_ch: 没想到您还是应了凯妮斯大人的邀请…真是太好了。
t_en: I didn't expect you to agree to Lady Caenis' invitation... What a wonderful turn of events.

t_ch: 说实话，对那位「金织」大人心怀怨言的人可不少……
t_en: To tell you the truth, there are many people who hold grudges against that Lady Goldweaver...

t_ch: 汝与那位「金织」互不对付，何不当面谈谈呢？
t_en: You seem to be avoiding that "Goldweaver" person. Why don't you address her face to face?

t_ch: 我跟她没什么好谈的…你在我脑子里，大可自己检索。
t_en: I have nothing to talk about with her... Since you're in my brain, you can look for the reason yourself.

t_ch: 哦，逐火之旅的领袖，墨涅塔的半神……
t_en: Oh... The leader of the Flame-Chase Journey and a demigod of Mnestia...

t_ch: 你还真检索啊，把我的脑子当成什么了？
t_en: You're really searching through my brain? What are you treating it as, some sort of toy?

t_ch: 尽管如此，汝对她的评价并不低，对吧？
t_en: Regardless, you think quite highly of her, no?

t_ch: 毕竟我是个打分公允的老师。
t_en: That's because I'm a teacher who grades everyone fairly.

t_ch: 来古士阁下，凯妮斯阁下，就在前面。
t_en: Lord Lygus and Lady Caenis are just ahead.

t_ch: 我先行告退了。
t_en: I'll take my leave for now.

t_ch: 诸位，欢迎来到「半神议院」。
t_en: Welcome to the Demigod Council, esteemed guests.

t_ch: 我曾有幸和你的老师恩贝多克利斯一同探讨「敬拜学派」的文章。
t_en: I was fortunate enough to have the opportunity to discuss a treatise about the Venerationists with your teacher, Empedocles.

t_ch: 但那刻夏阁下属于「智种学派」？我听闻那是反对者众多的一派。
t_en: Yet, it seems you subscribe to the Nousporists instead, Lord Anaxa? I've heard that this school of thought is one with many detractors.

t_ch: 有支持和反对很正常，就像世间也分贤才和笨货。
t_en: It's normal for ideas to have supporters and dissenters, just like how there are two types of people in this world — geniuses and dimwits.

t_ch: 在久远的神话里，{RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}曾在神山上回答信众们的发问。
t_en: A bygone myth tells of an occasion where {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#} once answered their followers' questions on the peak of the holy mountain.

t_ch: 所以，信众将登上泰坦断崖视为一种与神同行的方式。
t_en: Their followers have since believed that scaling this Titan cliff is one way of being with the god.

t_ch: 听见了吗？走吧，与{RUBY_B#理性之泰坦}瑟希斯{RUBY_E#}登山同行，机会难得，汝当吟唱赞歌。
t_en: Hear that? It's not every day that you get to climb a mountain with {RUBY_B#Reason Titan}Cerces{RUBY_E#}. You should sing me a hymn of praise.

t_ch: …我可不会唱什么泰坦赞歌。
t_en: ...Titan hymns are beyond me.

t_ch: （这是…亡灵……）
t_en: (This is a... departed spirit...?)

t_ch: 哈，又是你们……
t_en: Hah, you two again...

t_ch: 呵呵，无妨……
t_en: I'm fine...

t_ch: 如果真如我所想…那可就太有意思了。
t_en: If this is indeed as I thought... then this will be very amusing.

t_ch: 又见面了…你们应该不介意多个听众吧？
t_en: So, we meet again... I'm sure you wouldn't mind a snooping ear?

t_ch: 不见了…？
t_en: They're gone?

t_ch: （哼，算了…我一人也足够。）
t_en: (Hmph. Doesn't matter... I can do this myself.)

t_ch: （看来已经非常近了…死者的疆域。）
t_en: (Looks like I'm nearly there now... The land of the dead.)

t_ch: 「美丽的旧世界，如今在何处？」
t_en: "O beautiful old world, where are you now?"

t_ch: 「传说的黄金世，快回到人间！」
t_en: "O legendary Era Chrysea, return to the mortal realm!"

t_ch: 「光辉灿烂的众神曾行于我们所行之道，」
t_en: "Splendid gods once walked the paths we walk."

t_ch: 「哎，那温暖的一切只留下了幻影缥缈。」
t_en: "Alas, all that's left of that warmth is a hazy mirage."

t_ch: 你怎么也念起来了……
t_en: ...Why are you chanting that too?

t_ch: 那不是「穿着华服的大地兽」吗？
t_en: Isn't that the "dromas draped in finery"?

t_ch: …别对上眼了，我可不认识渎神者。
t_en: ...Avoid his gaze. I don't acquaint myself with blasphemers.

t_ch: 你放心，人家心高气傲，瞧不上咱们。
t_en: Don't worry, he's too full of pride to pay us any attention.

t_ch: 连外号也是大地兽？汝是真的喜欢大地兽。
t_en: Your nickname is "dromas" as well? You must really like those creatures.

t_ch: 恩贝多克利斯的学生？我听说他的观点和「敬拜学派」截然相反。
t_en: A student of Empedocles? I hear that his views run counter to the Venerationists.

t_ch: 学者是这样的，有不同才会有碰撞，有碰撞才会有进步。
t_en: This is what being a scholar is about: A collision of ideas only occurs when there are differences in beliefs, and it is such collisions that lead to progress.

t_ch: 但在我看来，只有正确的观点和错误的观点，二者不可共存。
t_en: Yet in my view, there are only right and wrong perspectives, neither of which can coexist with the other.

t_ch: 非黑即白，看来这就是人子们争来争去的原因。
t_en: What radical thinking... I guess this is why the children of humanity always squabble with each other.

t_ch: 没意思。
t_en: How boring.

t_ch: 在想什么？
t_en: What are you thinking about?

t_ch: 只是在感叹，这株参天巨树就是{RUBY_B#理性之泰坦}瑟希斯{RUBY_E#}的神躯？真是壮观。
t_en: Just amazed at how this towering tree is {RUBY_B#Reason Titan}Cerces{RUBY_E#}'s divine body. It's a majestic sight.

t_ch: 这样吗？我反倒觉得，你一点也不惊讶。感觉丹宝对「环绕大树的城池」很熟悉？
t_en: Is that so? You don't look the slightest bit amazed, though. Plus, you seem to be strangely familiar with the city around the Great Tree...?

t_ch: …只是些旧事，不展开了。
t_en: ...Merely something from the past that I won't go in to.

t_ch: 旧事啊…第一次仰望{RUBY_B#理性之泰坦}瑟希斯{RUBY_E#}时，我也是个孩子。它的枝叶反射着星星的光，躯干仿佛是天地的支柱。
t_en: Events of the past, huh... I was also a child when I looked up at {RUBY_B#Reason Titan}Cerces{RUBY_E#}. Their leaves and branches reflected the sparkling stars, and their trunk was like a pillar that supported the sky and ground.

t_ch: 当年，我就站在这里，幻想自己有朝一日能到树顶上去，那里是离「天空」最近的地方。
t_en: I stood at this very spot back then, imagining the day when I could climb to the top of this tree... Because I would be the closest I can be to the sky at that spot.

t_ch: 你身为「天空」祭司，却在「理性」泰坦的栖息地求学，似乎有些违背直觉。
t_en: You're a Sky Priest, yet you study at a place that is home to the Reason Titan... That's rather counterintuitive.

t_ch: 原因没那么复杂，凡人哪够得到{RUBY_B#天空之泰坦}艾格勒{RUBY_E#}的国度呢？它和瑟希斯不同，是严厉而不近人情的神明，「理性」愿与他人分享，但「天空」…只需要人们的敬畏。
t_en: Don't read too much into it. I mean, can mortals even hope to reach {RUBY_B#Sky Titan}Aquila's{RUBY_E#} realm? They're a strict and draconian god, nothing like {RUBY_B#Reason Titan}Cerces{RUBY_E#}. Reason is willing to share what is theirs with others, but Sky... only expects people to revere them.

t_ch: 丹宝也能理解吧，毕竟，把你和{NICKNAME}囚禁在翁法罗斯的泰坦…就是{RUBY_B#天空之泰坦}艾格勒{RUBY_E#}。
t_en: You get what I'm saying, right Dannie? After all, the Titan that imprisoned you and {NICKNAME} here in Amphoreus is none other than {RUBY_B#Sky Titan}Aquila{RUBY_E#} themself...

t_ch: 我也没想到，竟会在无意中成为一位本地神的眼中钉。
t_en: Yes, I didn't count on provoking the ire of a local god.

t_ch: 但总有地上的人怀抱着飞向天空的梦想。树庭不乏对{RUBY_B#天空之泰坦}艾格勒{RUBY_E#}的研究，这也是我们此行的目的。
t_en: But there will always be people who dream of soaring toward the sky. The Grove has a wealth of research on {RUBY_B#Sky Titan}Aquila{RUBY_E#}, and that is exactly what we are here for.

t_ch: 只是…临行前，灰宝却遇上了那种麻烦…我身为医师，本该陪在{M#他}{F#她}身旁的。
t_en: It's just... I never thought Grayie would meet with something like that before we set off... As a physician, I should have been there by {M#his}{F#her} side.

t_ch: 祸不单行也无可奈何。你还要为阿格莱雅查清树庭之灾的始末，总得走这一趟。路上多替{NICKNAME}留意下「死亡」泰坦的文献吧，也算不虚此行。
t_en: Bad luck tends to come in streaks, so don't beat yourself up over it. Besides, Aglaea needs you here to uncover the truth behind what happened to the Grove. Let's just make the most out of this trip and keep a lookout for any documents related to the Death Titan. They could be useful to {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 嗯，落在肩头的重担又多了一桩呢。
t_en: Yeah... There's no rest for the weary, huh.

t_ch: 偌大的树庭，不知要找的东西都分散在哪儿。我们分头行动吧，相信以丹宝的身手，黑潮造物也伤不到你。
t_en: In any case, what we're looking for could be anywhere in this enormous Grove. Let's split up to cover more ground. Dannie, with your skills, I'm sure those monsters born of the black tide will pose no threat to you anyway.

t_ch: 倒是你，没问题么？
t_en: What about you? Will you be alright on your own?

t_ch: 我可是「昏光庭院」的首席，你说这话，会不会太小瞧人家了？
t_en: Hehe, have you forgotten that you're talking to the chief physician of the Twilight Courtyard? Have more faith in me. I'll be just fine!

t_ch: …真安静啊。
t_en: ...The silence in here is near deafening.

t_ch: 这学府存放的知识堪比一座森林，最聪明的学者也会在其中迷路。出发前，要再整理下思路么？
t_en: With the vast amount of knowledge it holds, this academy is akin to a labyrinth of information... Even the cleverest of scholars would easily lose their way in here. I should gather my thoughts before heading in.

t_ch: 离开翁法罗斯的方法…
t_en: How to leave Amphoreus...

t_ch: （艾格勒，统治天空的泰坦。神话中，它被描述为一只长有百眼、俯瞰世界的巨鸟。只要它依旧飞翔于云端，翁法罗斯人就无法离开地面……）
t_en: (In the myths, Aquila, the Titan who reigns over the domain of the skies, is a colossal bird that overlooks the world beneath them with a hundred eyes. As long as they continue to soar above the clouds, the people of Amphoreus will remain trapped on the ground...)

t_ch: （风堇的先祖是信仰{RUBY_B#天空之泰坦}艾格勒{RUBY_E#}的祭司，她对「天空」的禁忌了解最深。另外，阿格莱雅派她做向导，也有为将来的试炼做准备的意思吧。）
t_en: (Hyacine's forebears were priests who worshiped {RUBY_B#Sky Titan}Aquila{RUBY_E#}. Nobody is more familiar with the taboos of the Sky Titan than her. Seeing how Aglaea has sent her here as a guide, it's possible that she plans to have Hyacine take on a trial in the future.)

t_ch: 翁法罗斯的泰坦神话…
t_en: Amphoreus' Titan mythology...

t_ch: （在翁法罗斯，「泰坦」是绕不开的话题。人们创造了神话，神话又塑造了他们…身在其中，总是很难保证客观。）
t_en: (In Amphoreus, Titans are a common topic of discussion. People create myths that, in turn, conceive Titans... It's a tricky task to keep an objective view when among the people of Amphoreus.)

t_ch: （有办法换个视角，跳出来思考么？）
t_en: (Is there a way to step out of the situation and look at it from a different perspective?)

t_ch: 泰坦与星神…
t_en: Titans and Aeons...

t_ch: （泰坦与星神，二者截然不同。前者能与人对话，能被人战胜，甚至能在陨落后，由人类接替神职。）
t_en: (Titans and Aeons are two distinct classes of beings. Titans can communicate with us humans, and it is not impossible for us to overpower them. Upon their demise, we can even take over their divinity.)

t_ch: （这么一看，和遥远的星神相比，泰坦与人类的距离…要近得多。）
t_en: (In this regard, Titans can be said to be a lot more similar to humans than Aeons are...)

t_ch: 黄金裔与半神…
t_en: The Chrysos Heirs and demigods...

t_ch: （黄金裔是具备英雄资质的人，大多身手不凡，拥有超人的力量。而通过火种试炼的黄金裔，更会荣升「半神」，接管泰坦的权能……）
t_en: (Chrysos Heirs are humans who possess heroic qualities, with most of them demonstrating extraordinary combat prowess and strength. Upon clearing a Coreflame trial, a Chrysos Heir can even ascend to a demigod and inherit a Titan's divinity...)

t_ch: （奇怪，总有一种违和感。这是什么…筛选机制么？）
t_en: (Wait, something about that feels off. Is this a... selection process of some sort?)

t_ch: 逐火之旅与神谕…
t_en: The Flame-Chase Journey and the prophecy...

t_ch: （在神谕的指引下，英雄们走在猎取火种的道路上，以「再创世」为目标，迄今为止，已经取得了不少成果。）
t_en: (Guided by the prophecy, the heroes are on a quest to reclaim Coreflames and usher in the Era Nova. Their efforts have seen gratifying results to date.)

t_ch: （果然，最难以解释的还是这道「神谕」。但眼下线索太少，思考也得不出结果，先搁置一旁吧。）
t_en: (As I expected, the prophecy is the hardest to make any sense of. But with barely any information to go on, no matter how much I ruminate on it, it won't yield any results... I should leave this for another time.)

t_ch: 还是想点别的…
t_en: Think of something else...

t_ch: （还是换个问题吧。）
t_en: (I should probably turn my attention to something else.)

t_ch: {NICKNAME}的现状…
t_en: {NICKNAME}'s plight...

t_ch: （{NICKNAME}…谁能想到，早在降落翁法罗斯时，{F#她}{M#他}就已经遭遇不测。）
t_en: (Nobody could've ever imagined that {NICKNAME}'s life would be forfeited the moment we landed on Amphoreus...)

t_ch: （没能保护好{F#她}{M#他}，是我的失职…但还有一线希望。翁法罗斯独特的死生规则，如今反倒成了救命稻草。）
t_en: (I should have done more to keep {M#him}{F#her} safe... but it's not too late. We still have a fighting chance. The unique rules that govern life and death on Amphoreus might just work in our favor and save {M#him}{F#her}.)

t_ch: （以树庭藏书之广博，会有类似记载吗？希望有吧……）
t_en: (The Grove is a veritable library of knowledge... Maybe they'll have records on a similar case? Please let there be one...)

t_ch: 不多想了，走吧。
t_en: Enough musing for now. It's time to move.

t_ch: 时间紧迫，还是尽快动起来吧。且行且看。
t_en: The clock is ticking... I should search for clues as I move.

t_ch: 黑色的怪物姿态扭曲，生命已随着灵魂一同化作死灰，无法辨认其生前的模样。
t_en: Black monsters are twisted in form, and their life has turned to ash together with their souls. It's impossible to figure out their form when they were still alive.

t_ch: 黑潮造物的遗骸。近距离看，的确和泰坦眷属有所不同。
t_en: These are... the remains of a black tide monster. It does look different from the Titankin from up close.

t_ch: 它的身下…压着什么东西？卷轴么。
t_en: What's that underneath it...? A scroll?

t_ch: 果然，谈论「天空」在树庭算不上禁忌。这两位学者在争论的「天舟」，记得阿格莱雅审讯时也提到过，不是什么愉快的回忆。
t_en: As I expected, the Grove doesn't forbid discussions about the Sky. It's a debate between two scholars about the skyships... Aglaea also made mention of the ships when she interrogated us... a memory I'd rather forget.

t_ch: 先收着这份记录吧。希望此行不会空手而归。
t_en: I'll hold onto this for now. With any luck, this journey won't leave us empty-handed.

t_ch: 风堇…？怎会有那么多敌人？…虽然没有小看她的意思，但还是去搭把手吧。
t_en: That's... Hyacine? Why is she surrounded by so many monsters? ...As much faith as I have in her, it's probably a good idea to help her out there.

t_ch: 哎呀，这可真是…麻烦远比想象得多呢。
t_en: Really now? This is way more trouble than I could've asked for.

t_ch: 要帮忙么？
t_en: Need a hand?

t_ch: 嗯…或者，就这么看着也可以？
t_en: Hm... Or you could sit back and watch?

t_ch: …一时分不清你是开玩笑还是认真的，这也是深不可测的一种体现吧。
t_en: I can't tell if you're serious or not... But being hard to read isn't without its perks, I suppose.

t_ch: 当然是开玩笑的。拜托啦丹宝，我们上吧——
t_en: Hehe, I'm kidding, Dannie! Lend me a hand, pretty please? Alright, now let's get them!

t_ch: 发生什么了？这里已是一片废墟，不该有这么多敌人才对。
t_en: This place is practically in ruins. What happened? There shouldn't be this many monsters here.

t_ch: 可能…是因为我吧。黑潮对翁法罗斯的「光明」格外敏锐，也许是天空祭司的赐福吸引了它们。
t_en: The black tide is remarkably quick to detect the light of Amphoreus, so I... or rather, the blessings of the Sky Priest in me, might have unknowingly called them to me.

t_ch: 先不说这个，给——我在附近发现的，你可能会感兴趣。
t_en: Anyway, never mind that for now. Here, you might want to have a look at this. I found it around here and thought it might be of interest to you.

t_ch: 阿那克…萨戈拉斯，是那位与「理性」火种合为一体的学者。
t_en: Anaxa... goras. He's the scholar who fused himself with the Coreflame of Reason.

t_ch: 连我也不知道，原来那刻夏老师进行过「天空」的研究。他从未提起此事。
t_en: This is new. I didn't know Professor Anaxa was researching the Sky. He's never mentioned anything about it.

t_ch: 听白厄说，他可能会站在逐火之旅的对立面。
t_en: Phainon said Anaxa might be opposed to the Flame-Chase Journey.

t_ch: 说「可能」有些委婉了。老师一向不敬神明，对神谕也持否定态度，即便在自由的树庭，他也被视作异端。
t_en: "Might" is an understatement. Anaxa has never been one to venerate the gods, nor has he ever found it within himself to put any faith in the prophecy. People have come to think of him as a heretic, even in a place as liberal as the Grove.

t_ch: 但偏偏是这样一位「渎神者」，却成了{RUBY_B#理性之泰坦}瑟希斯{RUBY_E#}的神选。命运真是捉摸不透啊。
t_en: Yet, it is also this very "blasphemer" that {RUBY_B#Reason Titan}Cerces{RUBY_E#} favors. Fate sure works in mysterious ways.

t_ch: 我在路上也找到一份卷轴，似乎和你手中的有些关联。
t_en: I picked up a scroll on my way here, too. It looks like it may be related to the one you're holding.

t_ch: 是吗？让我看看。
t_en: Oh? Let me have a look.

t_ch: 风堇将两份记录拼凑到一起……
t_en: Hyacine combines both records together...

t_ch: 看来这两份记录，是同一场景下的前后两段对话。
t_en: By the looks of them, these scrolls are records of two conversations that took place in the same setting, one after the other.

t_ch: 我对树庭还不够了解，方便问几个问题么？
t_en: There's still much I don't know about the Grove. Do you mind if I ask you a few questions?

t_ch: 请随意。
t_en: Sure, go ahead.

t_ch: 「敬拜学派」是…
t_en: Who are the Venerationists?

t_ch: 神悟树庭有七大学派，虽然都是围绕「最初的学者」塞勒苏斯的理念建立的，但彼此间各有不同，其中「敬拜学派」是最为敬奉泰坦的一支。
t_en: The Grove of Epiphany is home to seven schools of thought. While all seven schools are founded on the ideologies of Thalesus the First Scholar, each is unique in its own way. Among them, the Venerationists revere and worship the Titans the most.

t_ch: 尤绪弗罗老师是「敬拜学派」的贤人，总是和那刻夏老师针锋相对。
t_en: As the sage of the Venerationists, Euthyphro has always been at loggerheads with Anaxa.

t_ch: 如果把七贤人都放到{RUBY_B#律法之泰坦}塔兰顿{RUBY_E#}的天平上，一端是那刻夏，另一端一定得放上尤绪弗罗才能保持平衡吧。
t_en: If we were to place the Seven Sages on the scales of {RUBY_B#Law Titan}Talanton{RUBY_E#}, Anaxa and Euthyphro would be placed on opposite sides to achieve balance.

t_ch: 很形象的描述，我完全理解了。
t_en: A very illustrative explanation. Thank you.

t_ch: 建造天舟是错误的？
t_en: Was it a mistake to build the skyships?

t_ch: 如今，几乎每一个翁法罗斯人都知道「天舟」的故事。{RUBY_B#天空之泰坦}艾格勒{RUBY_E#}的身躯比天空更伟岸，它的神罚是如此无情。
t_en: Nearly everyone in Amphoreus has heard the story of the skyships by now. {RUBY_B#Sky Titan}Aquila{RUBY_E#}'s stature is loftier than the heavens, and their divine retribution is merciless.

t_ch: 所以，建造大型航天器是行不通的。嗯…不知道威力乘以一百倍的缇宝小火筒能不能骗过它的眼睛。
t_en: That's why it's no use building giant sky-faring ships... Oh, but I do wonder if a supercharged mini Tribbie cannon could slip past their watch?

t_ch: 这只是开玩笑，对吧。
t_en: That was a joke... right?

t_ch: 那刻夏的研究方向是…
t_en: What is Anaxa's field of study?

t_ch: 「智种学派」，核心课题是藉由炼金术达成对「灵魂」的修补。
t_en: Nousporists primarily study the application of alchemy in soul repair.

t_ch: 虽然被视作渎神者，但那刻夏本人从不避讳宣扬理念。要说有什么秘密…也只有他眼罩下的样子了。
t_en: Everyone considers Anaxa a blasphemer, but that has never stopped him from sharing his ideas, no matter how taboo. If you must say that he's hiding something... then it's likely what lies beneath his eye patch.

t_ch: 就这些问题。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 所有「错误」的尝试，都是更接近「正确」的一步…老师常把这句话挂在嘴边。
t_en: Anaxa often says that... "Every failure brings us one step closer to the truth."

t_ch: 也许在他看来，天舟的坠毁具有另一重意义：天幕并非不可突破，正因它迈出了关键的一步，才会招致神罚。
t_en: Perhaps, to him, the crash of the skyship carries a different interpretation. The firmament is not impenetrable. On the contrary, it was precisely because the skyship represented the first step toward breaking free from the shackles of the earth that it provoked the wrath of the Sky Titan and brought divine punishment upon itself.

t_ch: 可惜，树庭对{RUBY_B#天空之泰坦}艾格勒{RUBY_E#}的研究仍以「敬拜学派」为主，大多是些祭祀的仪式。对更外侧的探讨，在天舟一事后越发式微，连这份卷轴我也是第一次看见。
t_en: It's a shame that the majority of the Grove's research on {RUBY_B#Sky Titan}Aquila{RUBY_E#} was headed by the Venerationists, with sacrificial rituals making up a predominant part of their findings. Discourse about more esoteric subject matters in relation to {RUBY_B#Sky Titan}Aquila{RUBY_E#} dwindled even further after the skyship incident, so much so that even this scroll is completely new to me.

t_ch: 没关系，眼下先回收针对泰坦的研究就好。
t_en: Don't worry about it. We'll just collect everything we can find on the Titans for now.

t_ch: 我更好奇另一件事：这里提到，那刻夏在进行某种秘密实验？
t_en: There's something else that's caught my attention, though... This says that Anaxa is conducting secret experiments of some sort?

t_ch: 我也很在意…有什么研究，是连我这个讲师助理都不知情的？
t_en: That's what I'd like to know. What are these experiments he's involved in? Even I, the assistant lecturer, know next to nothing about them...

t_ch: 这里是树庭的藏书处，友爱之馆。
t_en: This is the Library of Philia, a place where the Grove stores its collection of tomes.

t_ch: 七大学派的著述此处均有收藏，还囊括了外邦的哲学、诗歌、信仰书籍…据说向树庭求取智慧的城邦，都要献上等价的知识。
t_en: Other than books written by the seven schools, you can also find philosophical texts, ballads, and religious writings from the outlands here. Cities that desired to learn from the Grove were apparently expected to contribute knowledge of equal value to what they sought.

t_ch: 历史上还发生过不少令人哭笑不得的事。据说「赤陶学派」曾向某位作家借取原稿，最后归还的却是副本，而真迹则被悄悄藏在这里。
t_en: We've had more than our fair share of hilarious episodes over the years. For instance, it's said that the Erythrokeramists once borrowed a manuscript from a certain author but ended up returning a copy instead of the original! What's more, the original manuscript was supposedly then hidden here.

t_ch: 这…似乎有些无礼。
t_en: That... doesn't seem like a nice thing to do.

t_ch: 嗯？不用这么委婉的…简直是强盗一般的行径呢！
t_en: Nice? No need to hold yourself back, Dannie. You can just tell it like it is: Their behavior was no different from bandits!

t_ch: 那，树庭「打劫」过悬锋城么？
t_en: Well, if you don't mind: Did the Grove ever rob Castrum Kremnos?

t_ch: 这我就不知道了。有人说悬锋城连字典都没有，也许有幸逃过一劫吧。
t_en: You've got me there. Some said you couldn't even find a dictionary in Castrum Kremnos, so I guess they were spared.

t_ch: 友爱之馆的书目过于庞杂，一卷卷翻阅不现实。我们用藏书石版检索出需要的资料，统一带回奥赫玛研究吧。
t_en: There are too many books in the Library of Philia. Flipping through them all will take too long. Why don't we use the biblioslate to search for the texts we need? We can bring them back to Okhema for reading after that.

t_ch: 此地危险，也不适宜久留。
t_en: Agreed, this place isn't safe. We should leave as soon as we're done.

t_ch: 地上还有些散落的卷轴。在你操作时，我四下检查一遍。这样应该不会有遗漏了。
t_en: There are still several more scrolls strewn all over the place. I'll gather them while you pull up those texts, this way, nothing will be overlooked.

t_ch: 一本被众多学者密密麻麻批注过的书，书名为《诡辩术：提升辩论技巧的四十种方法》。
t_en: A book covered in annotations from many scholars. Its title is The Art of Sophistry: 40 Ways to Improve Your Debating Skills.

t_ch: 大致翻阅了一遍，被重点圈出的方法包括虚拟原因、虚假前提、矛盾转换、名实混一、讹误断句、移花接木、以全概偏等等。
t_en: You skim through it, and the highlighted methods include virtual causation, false premises, contradiction conversion, conflating prevarication and reality, misinterpreted punctuation, misattribution, and generalizing specificity, among others.

t_ch: 其中夹杂着学者们的感叹：「醍醐灌顶了，语言的艺术学无止境」，「学不会诡辩，至少可以学点诚实之人的的防身术」，等等。
t_en: Amidst it are scholars' exclamations: "A true eye-opener, the art of language knows no bounds," "If I can't master sophistry, I can at least learn to defend myself against them as an honest man," and so on.

t_ch: 最后一条方法是…「实在不行，付诸武力」。
t_en: The last resort is... "When all else fails, employ brute force."

t_ch: …不是什么有价值的信息。
t_en: ...This isn't going to be of any help, that's for sure.

t_ch: 一名「山羊学派」学者申请的多个研究课题，及学派贤人对其研究课题的回复结果……
t_en: A record of the many research topics a Caprist scholar had applied for, and the replies from their school's Sage about these topics...

t_ch: 课题一：发现许多以寿命长著称的奇兽实际上生活在奇兽出生和死亡记录管理不善的地方，申请研究。回复：缺乏研究意义，暂不批准。
t_en: Topic 1: A discovery that many mythical beasts renowned for their longevity actually reside in areas with poorly managed birth and death records for such creatures. Permission to research. Reply: Lacks research significance, not approved.

t_ch: 课题二：树庭内有真正的植物在模仿相邻近的仙女木的生长趋势，申请研究。回复：这是「莲食学派」的研究方向，暂不批准。
t_en: Topic 2: An observation of real plants within the Grove imitating the growth patterns of nearby White Dryads. Permission to research. Reply: Falls under the domain of the Lotophagist school, not approved.

t_ch: 课题三：将会飞的鸟儿置入火筒以指导火筒飞行方向的可行性，申请研究。回复：过于异想，暂不批准。
t_en: Topic 3: Investigating the feasibility of placing flying birds into rocket tubes to guide the direction of the rocket's flight. Permission to research. Reply: Too fanciful, not approved.

t_ch: …接下来的课题越来越五花八门，唯有回复的结果不变。
t_en: ...The subsequent topics grew increasingly eclectic, yet the outcome of the replies remained unchanged.

t_ch: 这种锲而不舍的精神…但愿能帮助他从黑潮中幸存。
t_en: Impressive tenacity... Hopefully it helped him survive the black tide.

t_ch: 这是众学者们针对「渎神者」阿那克萨戈拉斯的投诉信，积累了厚厚一沓。
t_en: These are complaint letters from the scholars about Anaxagoras "The Blasphemer." There's a thick stack of letters.
